design_philosophy.core_loop: Select historical fighter character → Battle through tournament ladder of 14 opponents → Defeat two final bosses (Neo Geegus, Dio) to prove yourself as strongest fighter in history
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Character matchup execution within best-of-three rounds; learning opponent patterns and adapting combos and special moves in real-time
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become the ultimate historical fighter by defeating an eclectic cast of famous warriors and figures from across time periods
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately anachronistic character roster (historical figures as arcade fighters) with environmental hazard-based Death Match mode that uses novel 'seesaw lifebar' mechanic instead of standard health bars, forcing risk/reward positioning decisions

difficulty.continues_system: Standard arcade continue system (implied by arcade cabinet format)
difficulty.checkpointing: Staged tournament progression: defeat 6 new characters, then 4 randomly-selected original characters, then 2 boss fights to complete
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Death Match mode optional alternative with different hazard-based stage design and modified lifebar rules; player selects difficulty implicitly through mode choice

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament bracket structure: advance by winning best-of-three matches against progressively ordered opponents; bosses (Neo Geegus, Dio) only accessible after defeating prerequisite fighters
replayability_hooks: Random selection of 4 out of 8 original characters in mid-tournament creates variable opponent lineups
Death Match mode with environmental hazards and seesaw lifebar provides alternate challenge path
14-character roster with distinct move sets encourages character switching and mastery

world_building_techniques: Character roster spanning historical periods (Jeanne D'Arc, Rasputin, Kotaro Fuuma, etc.) establishes anachronistic 'historical tournament' premise
Plot device of Dr. Brown's World Heroes tournament framing narrative explains matchup context
Recurring villain Geegus/Neo Geegus creates narrative through-line across series entries

boss_design.philosophy: Two-stage finale: semi-final boss (Neo Geegus, returning antagonist from prior game) followed by true final boss (Dio). Boss encounters follow standard best-of-three round structure with no documented special mechanics
notable_bosses: Neo-Geegus (semi-final boss, returning villain from World Heroes 1)
Dio (final boss, introduced in World Heroes 2)

environmental_storytelling: Death Match stages feature environmental hazards reflecting thematic stage design
Character move sets and aesthetics communicate historical/cultural identity
